title: The 10 Best Action Fantasy Series on Netflix, Ranked

blocks.content: You'll never find a dearth of content to watch on Netflix. It's a streamer with a seemingly endless cache of content, making it difficult to choose what to watch. You're settling in for a weekend binge, you've got your popcorn, you're wearing your cozy PJs ... but you find yourself staring blankly at the Netflix home page. After a few minutes of silence that feels like they stretch into eternity, you narrow it down to the genre (or, rather, subgenre): action fantasy. Thankfully, the streamer has plenty of shows that fit the bill. From Shadow and Bone to Locke & Key, there's something in the action fantasy space for everyone.

I've compiled a list of 10 Netflix action fantasy series, ranked from not-so-great to really, really great, to help you figure out what to watch next. I singled out the below for their intricate world-building, unique magic systems, immersive visuals, and high production values. So, without further ado, let's delve into the best of what Netflix has to offer in this subgenre.

Why You Should Trust Me: I've been covering fantasy and sci-fi for nine years as a freelance entertainment journalist, specifically in the realm of TV. My primary coverage on MovieWeb has been Game of Thrones-related, but I'm a sucker for anything with magic.
10 'The Witcher'
It's Henry Cavill Killing Monsters – What More Could You Want?
https://static0.moviewebimages.com/wordpress/wp-content/uploads/2026/05/the-witcher.jpg?q=49&fit=crop&w=825&dpr=2

Watch This If: Okay, okay, before you start throwing tomatoes at me, hear me out. The Witcher adaptation, which brings the popular games and Andrzej Sapkowski's novels to life, has been fraught with issues. Henry Cavill's departure after three seasons as the perfect Geralt of Rivia essentially cut the action fantasy series off at the knees. However, the first two seasons are a total blast. At the very least, watch everything prior to Season 3 for an action-packed, magical, high-octane, immersive experience. If anything, the intricate lore baked into the DNA of The Witcher's first half might ignite interest in Sapkowski's books. Is it a perfect adaptation, even with Cavill? No, but it's so much more enjoyable.
Yes – the fifth and final season will debut in 2026

Skip This If: You don't want to watch the lead actor hand off the baton over halfway through the show's run. It's jarring, to be sure, and Liam Hemsworth gives it the old college try, but it's not the same. The overall quality of the series nosedives significantly in Season 4, and it feels hollow.
9 'Avatar: The Last Airbender'
High Production Values Meet Unique Elemental Abilities
https://static0.moviewebimages.com/wordpress/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/gordon-cormier-as-aang-in-avatar-the-last-airbender.jpg?q=49&fit=crop&w=825&dpr=2

Watch This If: You crave escapism with gorgeous visuals and fun powers. Like The Witcher, it's not perfect, and if you're comparing it to the animated series of the same name, you'll be disappointed. However, Netflix's live-action Avatar: The Last Airbender boasts high production values that really bring this world to life. The costumes and magical aspects are breathtaking, as are the locations. Gordon Cormier makes for a delightful Aang, and I'm excited to see Toph in Season 2. If magic, especially related to the elements, is your thing, give this series a whirl.
Yes – the second season debuts on June 25, 2026

Skip This If: You love the humor of Nickelodeon's animated series. The live-action version has some humor, but it doesn't have as many moments of levity as the former. The bar is through the roof when one is adapting a beloved cult classic, so if you feel you'll nitpick more than enjoy the show, sit this one out.
8 'Locke & Key'
A Magical Adaptation That Blends Dark Fantasy, Horror, Comedy, and Drama
https://static0.moviewebimages.com/wordpress/wp-content/uploads/2026/05/locke-and-key.jpg?q=49&fit=crop&w=825&dpr=2

Watch This If: You love fantasy action series with a grounded, human element at the core of its narrative. This adaptation of the Joe Hill and Gabriel Rodriguez comic book series of the same name has a profoundly relatable hook: grief. The titular Locke siblings and their mother are in a tailspin following the mysterious murder of their father. Locke & Key keeps its finger on that pulse throughout its three-season run. The lore behind the keys is fascinating, and its unique world-building is perfect if you're on the hunt for something outside your run-of-the-mill genre series.
No, but the series ended on its creators' terms

Skip This If: Deviations from the source material or tonal shifts are hard for you to sit through. I'm usually forgiving of deviations because, let's face it, TV is a different medium, but I understand that this show's more CW-esque tone can be a turnoff.
7 'Wednesday'
Perfect for the Creepy and the Kooky (With That Tim Burton Flair)
https://static0.moviewebimages.com/wordpress/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/wednesday-forest.jpg?q=49&fit=crop&w=825&dpr=2

Watch This If: Wednesday isn't quite as dark as I was expecting, but it still has that pinch of '90s Tim Burton flair that makes it a macabre delight. Jenna Ortega is the shining star here – she steers this Gothic ship with non-blinking, sardonic aplomb. The visuals are perfect if you long to escape to a place where magical misfits are accepted. Ortega injects the Addams Family's darkest storm cloud with nuance and humanity beneath all that stoicism. This series marries fantasy and action for an entertaining viewing experience.
Yes – the third season is slated for release in 2027

Skip This If: You want something less family-friendly and much, much darker. This isn't an adult series, and the horror elements embedded in the fantasy aren't particularly gory or riddled with jump scares.
6 'Castlevania'
A Dark Fantasy With Sumptuous Visuals
https://static0.moviewebimages.com/wordpress/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/castlevania-perfect-fantasy-action-series-streaming-netflix.jpg?q=49&fit=crop&w=825&dpr=2

Watch This If: This Netflix series adapts the Japanese video game series of the same name, and it certainly lives up to the "action fantasy" label here. Castlevania's animation is perfect for anime fans, and it's easily the best part of the series. Its massive voice cast, chock-full of genre staples like Graham McTavish, James Callis, Richard Armitage, and the late Lance Reddick, also makes Castlevania worth a watch. It's not a perfect adaptation, but it's visually engrossing and perfectly taps into those dark, spooky vibes if that's what you're craving.
No, but the series ended on its creators' terms

Skip This If: You want a faithful adaptation to the last frame. While Season 1 is the closest to the games, it diverges from there and becomes its own thing. However, if you can view the games and the series as two entities, perhaps this is up your alley.
5 'The Sandman'
A Dreamy, Fantastical Trip Through a Singular World
https://static0.moviewebimages.com/wordpress/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/the-sandman-season-2.jpg?q=49&fit=crop&w=825&dpr=2

Watch This If: You think a villain with teeth for eyes is hot. In all seriousness, Boyd Holbrook gives a generational performance as The Corinthian, The Sandman's antagonist. This Netflix adaptation (there are a lot of those on this list) is a heady, dizzying trip, complete with eye-catching visuals (there's that eye thing again), high production values, thought-provoking themes, and compelling performances. Tom Sturridge makes for the perfect Morpheus – teenage Melody would've fallen head over heels for him during her emo phase.
No – it was canceled in January 2025 before its final season premiered
Skip This If: Creator Neil Gaiman seems like a not-so-good person, so if the allegations surrounding him cast too long a shadow over this series, perhaps let this one pass you by.
4 'Shadow and Bone'
An Intricately Detailed Magic System, Bolstered by Solid Visuals
https://static0.moviewebimages.com/wordpress/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/1-5.jpg?q=49&fit=crop&w=825&dpr=2

Watch This If: Young adult adaptations are perennial. Leigh Bardugo's YA Grishaverse series comes to life with Netflix's Shadow and Bone, and what really stands out to me is its meticulous magic system. Bardugo's world is so layered and fully realized, and the adaptation captures this well. The chemistry between Jessie Mei Li's Alina Starkov and Ben Barnes' General Kirigan is off the charts, too. So, if you like unique magic complemented by fun character dynamics and some good old-fashioned tension that you can cut with a rusty butter knife, give this one a watch.
No – Netflix canceled the series and its planned Six of Crows spin-off
Skip This If: You don't want to watch a series that ends on a massive cliffhanger. Unfortunately, you won't get the narrative resolution you crave. Justice for Shadow and Bone!
3 'Sweet Tooth'
A Genre-Defying Series That Tugs on the Heartstrings
https://static0.moviewebimages.com/wordpress/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/sweet-tooth.jpg?q=49&fit=crop&w=825&dpr=2

Watch This If: Netflix's Sweet Tooth is action fantasy with a heaping helping of heart. It blends dystopian sci-fi with adventure and a smattering of survivalist drama, bolstered by gripping performances throughout and solid world-building. The post-apocalyptic aspect of the virus will certainly resonate with anyone watching in this post-COVID world. Despite the grimness of this narrative, there's an inherently hopeful tone that abounds.
No, but the series ended on its creators' terms
Skip This If: You're on the hunt for a wholly faithful Sweet Tooth adaptation. The comics are quite different from the series. Also, if child-led shows in general are a turnoff.
2 'Arcane'
A Visual Feast for the Eyes
https://static0.moviewebimages.com/wordpress/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/arcane-vi.jpg?q=49&fit=crop&w=825&dpr=2

Watch This If: Arcane is yet another adaptation of a popular video game series. Set in Riot's League of Legends universe, the show is a delightful amalgam of dark fantasy, action, sci-fi, and steampunk. Its stellar voice cast is a boon, to be sure, which includes the likes of Hailee Steinfeld, Ella Purnell, Katie Leung, and Harry Lloyd. The color palette and innovative animation truly elevate the story, along with a bangin' soundtrack and captivating world-building. If you're on the hunt for something different that bends genre conventions, watch Arcane. It's a religious experience, especially Season 1.
No, but the series ended on its creators' terms

Skip This If: You're in a binge-watching mood and want more than two seasons to watch. Season 2's pacing is also not on par with its predecessor, and the storytelling falls a bit short, too. The series could've used another season to fully flesh out the overarching plot.
1 'One Piece'
A Wildly Colorful and Breezy Adaptation
https://static0.moviewebimages.com/wordpress/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/one-piece-netflix-02.jpg?q=49&fit=crop&w=825&dpr=2

Watch This If: One Piece is a delight from top to bottom. From its impressive production values to its costumes to its larger-than-life characters, this adaptation of the long-running anime is the pinnacle of action fantasy on Netflix. The creatives understand what kind of show this is – it's good old-fashioned, swashbuckling entertainment, and it doesn't take itself too seriously. Check this one out if you need something lighthearted and breezy to watch (with a pinch of piracy).
Yes – the third season is slated for 2027
Skip This If: Admittedly, I'm struggling to find a reason why you'd want to skip this one. Who hates camp? Who hates fun?
What are your favorite action fantasy shows on Netflix? Let us know in the comments.
